blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: The one doing the watching. Give me something on them.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I can give you the behavior in detail. Identity — no. Low light, moving target, thirty feet of distance. What I can read in those conditions is orientation and attention pattern. Not face, not build beyond general impression.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Seated. Closer to the edge of the group than the center. Still — the others shifted around, refilled drinks, turned to each other. This one didn't move. The stillness was part of it.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: That could be anyone in a group of six.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: It could. Which is why I'm telling you what I saw, not who I think it was.
Davenport looked at him steadily. The kind of look that takes someone's measure without announcing it.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Crane. Julian Crane. Behavioral analysis. I consult — mostly fraud, corporate investigations. Pattern recognition in how people act and what those patterns mean.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: So you read rooms for a living and you happened to walk past a bonfire on Friday night.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: The one being watched. Describe them.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Taller than the others. Sitting at the outer edge of the group, fire to one side. Still — but a different kind of still than the watcher. Relaxed. Unaware.
Davenport was quiet for a moment. Something in his expression shifted — just slightly.
